Notes.Text:
This update for yelp fixes the following issue

- CVE-2026-13601: overly permissive Content Security Policy allows host file disclosure via Flatpak applications (bsc#1269625).

Vulnerabilities.Description: A flaw was found in Yelp due to an overly permissive Content Security Policy (CSP) implementation provided by yelp-xsl. A malicious Flatpak application can open crafted help content through the OpenURI portal. By embedding an untrusted CSS stylesheet within a structured SVG document, attacker-controlled content can bypass Flatpak's intended sandbox isolation, allowing Yelp to evaluate local XML inclusions and disclose arbitrary user-readable host files through remote CSS resource requests. This may result in the unauthorized disclosure of sensitive information.
